raw_response:
I agree with the selection of using a lag screw (option A). One additional point supporting this decision is the versatility of lag screws in accommodating different types of bony defects and irregularities that might occur post-BSSO setback procedure. Their ability to create a tensioned connection between fragmented bones aids in bridging gaps effectively, thereby enhancing union rates and minimizing displacement risks even under varying anatomical conditions. **Answer:** A
